epics.title: Project Bootstrap & Core Infrastructure
epics.description: Set up the project structure, CLI entry point with Commander, Graph client wrapper with auth, config loading, and base error handling infrastructure.
epics.body:
# Epic 1: Project Bootstrap & Core Infrastructure

## Scope
Everything needed before any audit checks can be written. This is the foundation layer.

## Key Deliverables
1. **Project structure** — folder layout by security category (`src/checks/identity/`, `src/checks/data-protection/`, `src/checks/access-control/`, `src/checks/threat-protection/`)
2. **CLI entry point** — Commander-based CLI with `audit` command, `--output-dir` flag, version/help
3. **Graph client wrapper** — Thin abstraction over `@microsoft/microsoft-graph-client` with:
   - Auto-auth via `ClientSecretCredential`
   - `.env` config loading via dotenv
   - v1.0 and beta endpoint support
   - Pagination helper using built-in `PageIterator` (auto-follow `@odata.nextLink`)
4. **Config types** — `AuditConfig` interface (tenant info, output dir, selected categories)
5. **Error handling** — Skip-warn-summarize pattern: catch per-check errors, collect warnings, summarize at end
6. **Permission validator** — Pre-flight check that tests required Graph permissions before running audit

## Technical Notes
- Stack pinned in asset `h4lwfk3c`: Node 24, TS 5.9.3, pnpm, ESM with `nodenext`
- Auth pattern validated in asset `xm93kcst` — `.js` extension required on azureTokenCredentials import
- `directoryRoles` endpoint does NOT support `$top` (discovered during testing)
- App registration already created: `9a7c3ea1-9f7a-4489-9c69-3b8173e72a07`

## Validated Architecture (from spike h36zybs5)

### CLI Pattern (Commander 14)
```typescript
import { Command } from 'commander';
const program = new Command()
  .name('o365-audit')
  .description('Office 365 Security Audit Framework')
  .version('1.0.0');

program.command('audit')
  .option('--output-dir <dir>', 'Output directory', './output')
  .option('--categories <cats...>', 'Categories to audit')
  .action(async (options) => { /* ... */ });

program.parse();
```
- Async action handlers work natively — no extra wrappers needed
- Built-in TypeScript types sufficient (no `@commander-js/extra-typings` needed)

### Pagination Pattern (PageIterator)
```typescript
import { PageIterator, PageCollection } from '@microsoft/microsoft-graph-client';

async function getAllItems<T>(client: Client, endpoint: string): Promise<T[]> {
  const items: T[] = [];
  const response: PageCollection = await client.api(endpoint).get();
  const callback = (item: T) => { items.push(item); return true; };
  const pageIterator = new PageIterator(client, response, callback);
  await pageIterator.iterate();
  return items;
}
```
- SDK has built-in `PageIterator` — no manual nextLink handling
- Returns `false` from callback to pause, `isComplete()` to check

### Permission Validation
- Test auth with `GET /organization` (minimal permission)
- Then probe each required endpoint with `$top=1`
- Catch `403 Forbidden` → report missing permission by name
- Run before audit checks to fail fast

### Folder Structure
```
src/
├── index.ts                    # CLI entry point
├── config.ts                   # AuditConfig, .env loading
├── graph/
│   ├── client.ts               # Graph client wrapper + auth
│   └── pagination.ts           # PageIterator helper
├── checks/
│   ├── runner.ts               # Check executor with error collection
│   ├── types.ts                # CheckResult, CheckSeverity interfaces
│   ├── identity/               # MFA, CA policies, risky users
│   ├── data-protection/        # SharePoint sharing, DLP
│   ├── access-control/         # Admin roles, PIM
│   └── threat-protection/      # Safe links, anti-phishing
├── reports/
│   └── html-reporter.ts        # HTML report generator
└── utils/
    └── errors.ts               # Error collection/summary pattern
```

### dotenv 17.x
- ESM import unchanged: `import dotenv from 'dotenv'; dotenv.config();`
- Use `{ quiet: true }` to suppress injection log
- Quote values containing `#` in `.env` files

epics.title: Check Engine & Plugin Framework
epics.description: Build the check runner, plugin interfaces, auto-discovery system, result types (Finding/Severity), and framework mapping infrastructure.
epics.body:
# Epic 2: Check Engine & Plugin Framework

## Scope
The engine that discovers, runs, and collects results from audit checks. Must be complete before any checks are implemented.

## Key Deliverables
1. **Core types** — `CheckMetadata`, `CheckResult`, `Finding`, `Severity`, `FrameworkRef`, `CheckStatus`
2. **Check interface** — Function-based: each check exports `metadata` + `run(client, config)` async function
3. **Check discovery** — Auto-load all checks from category folders (`identity/`, `data-protection/`, etc.)
4. **Check runner** — Orchestrates: discover → validate permissions → run each → collect results → return summary
5. **Result aggregation** — Collect findings across all checks, group by category/severity/service
6. **Framework mapping** — `FrameworkRef` support in metadata (e.g. `CIS-M365-v6` control IDs)
7. **Timing & progress** — Track execution time per check, report progress to CLI

## Check Lifecycle
```
discover → filter (by category/service) → validate permissions → run → collect findings → aggregate
```

## Types Design (from asset vg60f2pl)
- `CheckMetadata`: id, title, description, category, service, severity, frameworks[], permissions[], remediation{}
- `Finding`: checkId, severity, title, detail, affected resources, remediation
- `CheckResult`: metadata, findings[], status (pass/fail/error/skip), duration, error?
- `AuditReport`: timestamp, tenant, results[], summary stats

successCriteria: Check types compile and are well-documented

research.content:
## Spike Results — Validated Design Decisions

**1. Auto-discovery**: Use native `node:fs/promises` `glob()` (Node 24) — no libraries needed. Pattern: `glob("dist/checks/**/check-*.js")` + dynamic `import()`.

**2. Plugin pattern**: Function-based exports (`export const meta` + `export async function run`) — simpler than class-based, confirmed working with ESM dynamic import.

**3. Aggregation**: Use native `Object.groupBy()` (Node 24) for grouping by severity/category/service.

**4. File convention**: Check files named `check-*.ts` in category subdirectories under `src/checks/`.

**5. Types refinement**: `CheckMeta` (with `service` and `frameworks[]`), `Finding` (resource + detail), `CheckResult` (status + message + findings), `CheckContext` (graphClient + config), `AuditReport` (timestamp + results + summary).

epics.closeMessage: Epic complete. Check engine fully implemented: core types (types.ts), auto-discovery (discovery.ts), runner with error handling and aggregation (runner.ts), and a dummy check validating the pipeline. All using Node 24 natives (glob, Object.groupBy). Framework mapping built into CheckMeta.frameworks[].

epics.title: Identity & Access Checks (Entra ID)
epics.description: Implement all Entra ID audit checks: privileged roles, MFA status, conditional access gaps, guest users, and app registrations/service principals.
epics.body:
# Epic 3: Identity & Access Checks (Entra ID)

## Scope
All 5 Entra ID checks — the largest check surface and highest security value. All use Graph v1.0 (with one beta endpoint for MFA state).

## Checks to Implement

### 1. Privileged Role Assignments
- `GET /directoryRoles` → for each role → `GET /directoryRoles/{id}/members`
- Flag: Global Admin count, users with multiple admin roles
- Permission: `RoleManagement.Read.Directory`
- Note: `/directoryRoles` does NOT support `$top`

### 2. MFA Enforcement Status
- `GET /users/{id}/authentication/methods` (v1.0) — list registered methods
- `GET /users/{id}/authentication/requirements` (**beta**) — per-user MFA state
- Flag: admins without MFA, users with no methods registered
- Permission: `UserAuthenticationMethod.Read.All`

### 3. Conditional Access Policy Audit
- `GET /identity/conditionalAccess/policies` (v1.0)
- Flag: disabled policies, policies not covering all users, no MFA requirement policies
- Permission: `Policy.Read.All`

### 4. Guest/External User Enumeration
- `GET /users?$filter=userType eq 'Guest'` (v1.0)
- Flag: guest count, guests with admin roles, stale guests (no recent sign-in)
- Permission: `User.Read.All`

### 5. App Registrations & Service Principals
- `GET /applications` — list all apps, check permissions, secret expiry
- `GET /servicePrincipals` — check granted permissions
- Flag: overprivileged apps (write permissions), expiring/expired secrets, apps with no owner
- Permission: `Application.Read.All`

research.content:
## Spike Results — Live Tenant Validation

All 5 endpoint groups tested. Key findings:
- **MFA**: Use v1.0 `authentication/methods` only — detect MFA by presence of non-password methods. No beta endpoint needed.
- **Guests**: `signInActivity` requires `AuditLog.Read.All` (not granted). Skip stale-guest for MVP.
- **Apps**: Secret expiry via `passwordCredentials[].endDateTime`. Permission count heuristic for overprivileged detection.
- **CA Policies**: 7 policies found. Gap = `state !== "enabled"` or missing MFA in `grantControls.builtInControls`.

epics.title: Data Protection Checks (Exchange + SharePoint)
epics.description: Implement Exchange Online checks (forwarding, delegates, inbox rules) and SharePoint/OneDrive checks (sharing settings, anonymous links, site permissions).
epics.body:
# Epic 4: Data Protection Checks (Exchange + SharePoint)

## Scope
Checks focused on data exfiltration and oversharing risks. Mix of v1.0, beta, and per-user iteration.

## Exchange Checks (validated via spike w0pc0m37)

### 1. Inbox Rules with Forwarding/Redirect
- `GET /users/{id}/mailFolders/inbox/messageRules` — check `actions.forwardTo`, `actions.redirectTo`, `actions.forwardAsAttachmentTo`
- Iterate all users (paginated); skip 404s (unlicensed/no mailbox)
- Flag: rules forwarding to external domains
- Permission: `MailboxSettings.Read` (already granted)
- **Note:** `mailboxSettings` does NOT expose forwarding addresses — forwarding detection must use messageRules

### 2. Mailbox Settings Audit
- `GET /users/{id}/mailboxSettings` — check `delegateMeetingMessageDeliveryOptions`, `automaticRepliesSetting`
- Flag: auto-replies enabled with external audience
- Secondary check only

## SharePoint / OneDrive Checks (validated via spike w0pc0m37)

### 3. Tenant Sharing Settings
- `GET /beta/admin/sharepoint/settings` — single call, no iteration needed
- Key fields: `sharingCapability`, `isResharingByExternalUsersEnabled`, `sharingDomainRestrictionMode`
- Flag: `sharingCapability` not `disabled` or `existingExternalUserSharingOnly`
- Permission: `SharePointTenantSettings.Read.All` (already granted)

### 4. Site Enumeration & Permissions
- `GET /beta/sites/getAllSites` — returns all sites including OneDrive personal sites
- `isPersonalSite` field distinguishes OneDrive vs team sites
- `GET /sites/{id}/permissions` — returns app-level permissions (empty for most sites)
- Site ID format: `hostname,siteGuid,webGuid`
- Permission: `Sites.FullControl.All`

### 5. Anonymous/Anyone Links
- Not directly available from tested endpoints
- May need `GET /sites/{id}/drive/root/children` + check sharing links
- Or SharePoint REST API — scope TBD

## Complexity Notes
- Exchange checks require iterating all users — 404s for unlicensed users are fast and expected
- SharePoint tenant settings are beta-only but stable
- Per-user calls ~200ms each, no rate limiting observed in small batches
- Calendar permissions (delegate access) requires `Calendars.Read` — not currently granted

**Relevant files:**
- `src/checks/data-protection/` — All data protection checks
- `src/graph/pagination.ts` — Pagination helper for per-user iteration

epics.title: Collaboration & Threat Checks (Teams + Exchange Policies)
epics.description: Implement Teams checks (guest access, app catalog) and Exchange policy checks. Clearly document PowerShell-only gaps as stub findings.
epics.body:
# Epic 5: Collaboration & Threat Checks (Teams + Exchange Policies)

## Scope
Teams and remaining Exchange checks. This epic has the most PowerShell-only gaps — implement what Graph covers and create clear stubs for the rest.

## Graph-Covered Checks (implement fully)

### 1. Teams Guest Detection
- `GET /teams` → `GET /teams/{id}/members` → filter `@odata.type` = `#microsoft.graph.aadUserConversationMember` with guest role
- Flag: teams with external guests, guest count per team
- Permission: `Team.ReadBasic.All`, `TeamMember.Read.All`

### 2. Teams App Catalog
- `GET /appCatalogs/teamsApps` — list installed/available apps
- Flag: third-party apps, sideloaded apps
- Permission: `AppCatalog.Read.All`

## PowerShell-Only Checks (implement as stubs)

### 3. Teams Meeting Policies (STUB)
- Requires: `Get-CsTeamsMeetingPolicy` via `MicrosoftTeams` PowerShell module
- Stub output: "This check requires PowerShell MicrosoftTeams module — not available via Graph API"
- What it would check: recording enabled, lobby bypass, anonymous join

### 4. Teams Guest Access Settings (STUB)
- Requires: `Get-CsTeamsGuestMeetingConfiguration`, `Get-CsTeamsGuestCallingConfiguration`
- Stub output with description of what it would check

### 5. Teams App Permission Policies (STUB)
- Requires: `Get-CsTeamsAppPermissionPolicy`
- Stub output

### 6. Exchange Transport Rules (STUB)
- Exchange Admin API preview exists but requires `Exchange Administrator` RBAC role on the service principal
- Stub with instructions on how to enable

### 7. Exchange Anti-Spam / Anti-Phish (STUB)
- Requires: `Get-HostedContentFilterPolicy`, `Get-AntiPhishPolicy` via `ExchangeOnlineManagement`
- No Graph API coverage whatsoever
- Stub output

## Stub Pattern
Each stub should produce a finding with:
- Severity: `info`
- Status: `skip`
- Clear message explaining WHY it's stubbed and WHAT PowerShell module is needed
- Remediation with the exact PowerShell command to run manually

epics.title: Reporting Engine (JSON + HTML Dashboard)
epics.description: Build the JSON output writer and HTML dashboard report with Chart.js — summary charts, severity breakdown, per-category detail tables, and remediation guidance.
epics.body:
# Epic 6: Reporting Engine (JSON + HTML Dashboard)

## Scope
Transform audit results into consumable outputs: structured JSON for tooling and a polished HTML dashboard for humans.

## Deliverables

### 1. JSON Output
- Write `AuditReport` to timestamped JSON file
- Structure: `{ timestamp, tenant, summary, results: [{ check, findings[] }] }`
- Pretty-printed, human-scannable
- Output path: `./output/{timestamp}/report.json`

### 2. HTML Dashboard Report
- Single self-contained HTML file (inline CSS + JS, Chart.js via CDN)
- **Dashboard sections:**
  - Executive summary: total findings by severity (Critical/High/Medium/Low/Info), pass rate
  - Severity breakdown chart (doughnut)
  - Findings by service chart (bar: Entra/Exchange/SharePoint/Teams)
  - Findings by category chart (bar: identity/data-protection/access-control/threat-protection)
  - Per-check detail table: check name, status, finding count, severity
  - Expandable finding details: affected resources, description, remediation (text + link + script)
- **Chart.js 4.5.1** via `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/chart.js@4.5.1/dist/chart.umd.js`
- Output path: `./output/{timestamp}/report.html`

### 3. Remediation Section
- Each finding includes:
  - Plain text remediation steps
  - Link to Microsoft documentation
  - Example PowerShell/CLI fix snippet (copy-pasteable)
  - CIS benchmark reference (if mapped)

### 4. Output Directory Structure
```
output/
  2026-03-12T14-30-00/
    report.json
    report.html
```

## Technical Notes
- HTML template can be a template literal in TypeScript — no need for handlebars/ejs
- Chart.js loaded from CDN — report works offline after first load
- No server needed — open HTML file directly in browser

epics.title: Drift Detection & CLI Polish
epics.description: Add local result caching for run comparison (new/resolved findings), CLI progress indicators, exit codes, and final UX polish for MVP release.
epics.body:
# Epic 7: Drift Detection & CLI Polish

## Scope
The final polish layer that makes the tool production-usable: drift detection between runs and CLI UX refinement.

## Deliverables

### 1. Local Result Cache
- Store each run's JSON report in `./output/{timestamp}/`
- Maintain a `latest` symlink or `./output/latest.json` pointer
- Keep configurable number of past runs (default: 10)

### 2. Drift Comparison
- Compare current run vs. previous run
- Report:
  - **New findings** — issues that appeared since last run
  - **Resolved findings** — issues that were fixed since last run
  - **Unchanged** — count of persistent findings
- Add drift summary to both JSON and HTML reports
- Finding identity: match by `checkId` + affected resource

### 3. CLI UX Polish
- Progress indicators during audit (which check is running, count/total)
- Summary table on completion (findings by severity, pass/fail counts)
- Exit codes: 0 = no critical/high, 1 = critical/high findings found, 2 = execution error
- `--verbose` flag for detailed per-check output
- `--category` filter to run only specific categories
- `--service` filter to run only specific services
- Color-coded terminal output (red = critical, yellow = medium, green = pass)

### 4. Final Touches
- README.md with usage instructions
- `.env.example` file
- `.gitignore` complete (dist/, output/, node_modules/, .env)

issues.title: Research & Asset Creation Process Guide
issues.description:
## Purpose
This issue documents the process used to research and create fact-checked technical assets for the M365 Audit Framework project. Use this to recover context if a session is lost.

## What Was Done

### Interview Process (6 rounds via questionnaire)
Captured all MVP requirements across: scope, services, auth, tech stack, output formats, risk scoring, benchmarks, execution model, check areas per service, module structure, API approach, error handling, config, data retention, testing, MVP boundaries, remediation detail, report style, check interface, and drift comparison.

### Assets Created

#### Project-Level (injected every session)
1. **`xq7yo25g`** — **Project Vision & Requirements**: Core scope, principles, MVP boundaries, out-of-scope
2. **`htjegbit`** — **Tech Stack & Architecture**: Stack choices, module design, API coverage reality, output/reporting
3. **`eyl0q5s2`** — **Safety & Compliance Policy**: Read-only mandate, permission model, no-secrets rule

#### Technical Reference (category: tech-stack)
4. **`xm93kcst`** — **Graph SDK & Auth Reference**: NPM packages, auth pattern code, .env config, app registration steps
5. **`1a8tuhf5`** — **Graph API Permissions & Endpoints Map**: Every check area mapped to endpoint + permission + API version. Includes consolidated permission list and PowerShell module requirements.
6. **`axsprcbe`** — **CIS M365 Benchmark Reference**: Version history (latest v6.0, Oct 2025, 140 controls), categories, L1/L2 levels, integration approach
7. **`vg60f2pl`** — **Audit Check Specification**: Check interface (TypeScript), category structure, full check list per service

### Key Technical Findings (web-researched, March 2026)
- `@microsoft/microsoft-graph-client` v3.0.7 is latest; use with `@azure/identity` and `@microsoft/microsoft-graph-types`
- **15 Graph application permissions** needed minimum for full MVP
- **Exchange Administrator** RBAC role needed for Exchange Admin API
- **Beta-only endpoints**: SharePoint tenant settings, per-user MFA state
- **No Graph API** for: Exchange anti-spam/anti-phish, transport rules (PowerShell), Teams meeting/guest/app policies (PowerShell)
- SharePoint site permissions require `Sites.FullControl.All` — no read-only alternative
- CIS M365 Benchmark v6.0.0 (Oct 2025) is current with 140 controls across 6 services

### How to Recover
1. Run `asset_list` to see all assets
2. Run `asset_show` on any asset ID for full content
3. The 3 project-level assets auto-inject into every session
4. Use `epic_list` / `issue_list` to see work items
5. This issue contains the full map of asset IDs and what they cover

issues.title: Spike: Validate bootstrap architecture and folder structure
issues.description:
## Goal
Expand on Epic 1 requirements by validating the project structure, Graph client wrapper design, and CLI setup. Research current best practices and ensure all documentation assets are up-to-date.

## Research Areas
1. Verify Commander 14 API for ESM/TypeScript — subcommand pattern, async action handlers
2. Validate Graph client pagination pattern — does SDK have built-in `PageIterator`? Or manual `nextLink`?
3. Confirm permission validation approach — can we call `GET /me` or similar to test token, then check `appRoleAssignments`?
4. Review folder structure conventions for plugin-style TS projects
5. Check if `dotenv` 17.x has breaking changes vs older patterns

## Deliverable
Updated epic description and research notes with validated patterns and any corrections to existing assets.

research.content:
## dotenv 17.x — No ESM Breaking Changes

- ESM import unchanged: `import dotenv from 'dotenv'; dotenv.config();`
- `#` in values now requires quoting: `SECRET="value-with-#-hash"`
- Multiline enabled by default (no flag needed)
- New: logs injection message on startup — silence with `dotenv.config({ quiet: true })`
- Our `.env` only has TENANT_ID, CLIENT_ID, CLIENT_SECRET — no `#` concerns

## Permission Validation Approach

For pre-flight permission check, use `GET /organization` (requires `Organization.Read.All` which is minimal):
- If token works → auth is valid
- Then test specific endpoints needed by selected audit categories
- Catch `403 Forbidden` → report which permission is missing
- Pattern: try each endpoint with `$top=1`, catch and collect errors

## Folder Structure — Validated

```
src/
├── index.ts                    # CLI entry point
├── config.ts                   # AuditConfig, .env loading
├── graph/
│   ├── client.ts               # Graph client wrapper + auth
│   └── pagination.ts           # PageIterator helper
├── checks/
│   ├── runner.ts               # Check executor with error collection
│   ├── types.ts                # CheckResult, CheckSeverity interfaces
│   ├── identity/               # MFA, CA policies, risky users
│   ├── data-protection/        # SharePoint sharing, DLP
│   ├── access-control/         # Admin roles, PIM
│   └── threat-protection/      # Safe links, anti-phishing
├── reports/
│   └── html-reporter.ts        # HTML report generator
└── utils/
    └── errors.ts               # Error collection/summary pattern
```

This follows plugin-style convention: each category folder contains independent check files that export a standard interface.

issues.title: Spike: Validate Entra ID Graph endpoints and response shapes
issues.description:
## Goal
Live-test all Entra ID Graph endpoints against the real tenant. Document actual response shapes, pagination behavior, and edge cases before implementing checks.

## Research Areas
1. `GET /directoryRoles` + `/directoryRoles/{id}/members` — response shape, which roles are activated
2. `GET /users/{id}/authentication/methods` (v1.0) vs `/authentication/requirements` (beta) — response differences, what methods look like for MFA vs no-MFA users
3. `GET /identity/conditionalAccess/policies` — full response shape, what a "gap" looks like (disabled, not covering all users)
4. `GET /users?$filter=userType eq 'Guest'` — confirm filter works with app-only, check `signInActivity` availability
5. `GET /applications` — secret expiry fields, permission grants shape, how to detect overprivileged apps
6. Cross-reference findings with CIS M365 v6 benchmark controls for Entra ID

## Deliverable
Research notes with actual API response samples from the live tenant. Updated epic with edge cases and corrections.

research.content:
## Live Tenant API Results

### 1. Directory Roles — ✅ Works
- `GET /directoryRoles` returns 30 activated roles
- Members via `/directoryRoles/{id}/members` — returns user objects with `displayName`, `userPrincipalName`, `id`
- No pagination issue (no `$top` support, returns all)

### 2. Authentication Methods — ✅ Works  
- `GET /users/{id}/authentication/methods` returns method objects with `@odata.type`:
  - `#microsoft.graph.passwordAuthenticationMethod`
  - `#microsoft.graph.phoneAuthenticationMethod`
  - `#microsoft.graph.softwareOathAuthenticationMethod`
  - `#microsoft.graph.microsoftAuthenticatorAuthenticationMethod`
- MFA detection: user has MFA if they have any non-password method

### 3. Conditional Access — ✅ Works
- `GET /identity/conditionalAccess/policies` returns 7 policies
- Key fields: `state` (enabled/disabled), `conditions.users`, `grantControls.builtInControls` (contains "mfa")
- Gap detection: check `state !== "enabled"`, missing MFA in grantControls

### 4. Guest Users — ⚠️ Partial
- `$filter=userType eq 'Guest'` works BUT `signInActivity` requires `AuditLog.Read.All` permission
- Fallback: enumerate guests without signInActivity, flag count and admin role membership only

### 5. Applications — ✅ Works
- `passwordCredentials[].endDateTime` for secret expiry detection
- `requiredResourceAccess[].resourceAccess[]` for permission analysis (type: "Role" = app permission)
- Need to resolve permission GUIDs to names via service principal lookup

### 6. Service Principals — ✅ Works
- Has `appRoles`, `oauth2PermissionScopes`, `passwordCredentials` for granted permission analysis

## Edge Cases & Design Notes

1. **MFA detection logic**: User has MFA if `authentication/methods` contains any type other than `passwordAuthenticationMethod`. No need for beta `/authentication/requirements` endpoint.

2. **Guest signInActivity**: Requires `AuditLog.Read.All` which we don't have. Skip stale-guest detection for now, focus on guest count + admin role membership.

3. **App permission resolution**: `requiredResourceAccess` contains GUIDs. To get human-readable names, cross-reference with Microsoft Graph service principal's `appRoles`. For MVP, flag apps with >5 Role-type permissions as potentially overprivileged.

4. **Secret expiry**: `passwordCredentials[].endDateTime` — flag if expired or expiring within 30 days.

5. **CIS M365 v3.1 mappings**:
   - Privileged roles → 1.1.1, 1.1.3
   - MFA → 5.2.2.1
   - Conditional access → 5.2.2.3
   - Guest users → 1.3.1
   - App registrations → 5.1

autoValidation.strategy: Verify live API calls were made and response shapes documented in research notes

issues.closeMessage: All Entra ID endpoints validated against live tenant. Response shapes documented, edge cases identified (guest signInActivity needs extra permission, MFA via v1.0 only). Epic updated with findings.

issues.title: Spike: Validate Exchange & SharePoint endpoints and response shapes
issues.description:
## Goal
Live-test all Exchange and SharePoint Graph endpoints. Document response shapes, per-user iteration performance, and beta endpoint stability.

## Research Areas
1. `GET /users/{id}/mailboxSettings` — response shape, where forwarding address lives, how to detect external forwarding
2. `GET /users/{id}/mailFolders/inbox/messageRules` — response shape, how forward/redirect actions look
3. Performance of per-user iteration — how many users before it becomes slow? Rate limiting?
4. `GET /beta/admin/sharepoint/settings` — full response shape (already partially tested), all relevant fields
5. `GET /sites` — does it return all sites? Pagination? How to get site-level sharing settings

8. Cross-reference with CIS M365 v6 data protection controls

## Deliverable

research.content:
## Endpoint Validation Results (Live Test)

### 1. `GET /users/{id}/mailboxSettings` ✅ Works
Response shape (key fields):
```json
{
  "archiveFolder": "...",
  "timeZone": "Romance Standard Time",
  "delegateMeetingMessageDeliveryOptions": "sendToDelegateOnly",
  "automaticRepliesSetting": { "status": "disabled", ... },
  "language": { "locale": "en-DK" },
  "workingHours": { ... }
}
```
**Important:** No `forwardingSmtpAddress` or `forwardingAddress` field appears in the response. Exchange forwarding is configured via Exchange transport rules or OWA, NOT exposed in Graph mailboxSettings. Need to use **inbox messageRules** to detect forward/redirect actions, or use Exchange PowerShell.

**404 errors** occur for users without Exchange mailboxes (no license). Must handle gracefully.

### 2. `GET /users/{id}/mailFolders/inbox/messageRules` ✅ Works
Returns `{ "value": [] }` when no rules. Each rule would have `actions` with `forwardTo`, `redirectTo`, `forwardAsAttachmentTo` arrays.

### 3. `GET /beta/admin/sharepoint/settings` ✅ Works
Key fields for audit:
- `sharingCapability`: `"externalUserSharingOnly"` — controls tenant-level sharing (values: `disabled`, `externalUserSharingOnly`, `externalUserAndGuestSharing`, `existingExternalUserSharingOnly`)
- `isResharingByExternalUsersEnabled`: `false`
- `sharingDomainRestrictionMode`: `"none"` (can be `allowList` or `blockList`)
- `sharingAllowedDomainList` / `sharingBlockedDomainList`: `[]`
- `isLegacyAuthProtocolsEnabled`: `false`
- `isSiteCreationEnabled`: `false`
- `idleSessionSignOut`: `{ "isEnabled": false, ... }`

### 4. `GET /sites` ✅ Works (with pagination)
- `GET /sites?$top=5` works, returns paginated list with `@odata.nextLink`
- `GET /sites?$search=*` does NOT work (400 error)
- `GET /beta/sites/getAllSites` ✅ works — returns all sites including personal (OneDrive) sites
- Sites have `isPersonalSite: true/false` to distinguish OneDrive vs team sites
- Site ID format: `hostname,siteGuid,webGuid`

### 5. `GET /sites/{id}/permissions` ✅ Works
Returns `{ "value": [] }` for root site. This shows app-level permissions on sites. For SharePoint group/user permissions, would need SharePoint-specific APIs.

### 6. Calendar permissions (delegate access) ❌ 403
`GET /users/{id}/calendar/calendarPermissions` returns 403. Need `Calendars.Read` permission (not currently granted). **Not blocking** — mailbox delegates can be checked via other means.

### Performance Notes
- Per-user mailboxSettings calls are fast (~200ms each)
- 404s for unlicensed users are fast
- Sites pagination works well with `$top` + `@odata.nextLink`
- Rate limiting not observed in small batch (5 users)

## Implementation Recommendations

1. **Exchange forwarding detection**: Use `messageRules` endpoint, look for rules with `actions.forwardTo`, `actions.redirectTo`, or `actions.forwardAsAttachmentTo` that target external domains. Must iterate per-user; skip 404s (unlicensed users).

2. **SharePoint sharing audit**: `GET /beta/admin/sharepoint/settings` gives all tenant-level settings in one call. Key check: `sharingCapability` should be `disabled` or `existingExternalUserSharingOnly` per CIS benchmarks.

3. **Site enumeration**: Use `GET /beta/sites/getAllSites` for complete list. Filter `isPersonalSite` to separate OneDrive from team sites. Per-site sharing settings may need SharePoint REST API (`_api/web/`) — Graph doesn't expose per-site sharing config directly.

4. **Anonymous links**: Not directly visible from tested endpoints. May need `GET /sites/{id}/drive/root/children` + check sharing links, or use SharePoint REST API.

5. **Error handling pattern**: Wrap per-user calls in try/catch, log 404s as "no mailbox" (info), 403 as permission error, other codes as warnings.

issues.title: Spike: Validate Teams endpoints and document PowerShell gaps
issues.description:
## Goal
Live-test Teams Graph endpoints and thoroughly document what PowerShell-only checks need. Research the stub pattern and exact PowerShell commands.

## Research Areas
1. `GET /teams` + `GET /teams/{id}/members` — response shape, how to identify guest members
2. `GET /appCatalogs/teamsApps` — response shape, how to distinguish first-party vs third-party vs sideloaded
3. Exchange Admin API preview — is `GetMailDetailTransportRuleReport` accessible with our app? Test it
4. Research exact PowerShell commands for each stub:
   - `Get-CsTeamsMeetingPolicy` — what fields to check
   - `Get-CsTeamsGuestMeetingConfiguration` — what fields matter
   - `Get-CsTeamsAppPermissionPolicy` — what to flag
   - `Get-HostedContentFilterPolicy` / `Get-AntiPhishPolicy` — key security fields
   - `Get-TransportRule` — key rule patterns to flag
5. Cross-reference with CIS M365 v6 Teams and Exchange controls

## Deliverable
Research notes with live response samples for Graph checks. Comprehensive stub documentation with exact PS commands and what each would check.

research.content:
## Graph API: Teams Endpoints

### 1. List Teams — `GET /teams`
- **App permission:** `Team.ReadBasic.All` (basic) or `Team.Read.All` (full, admin consent)
- **Response shape:**
```json
{
  "value": [
    { "id": "...", "displayName": "...", "description": "...", "visibility": "private|public", "webUrl": "...", "tenantId": "..." }
  ]
}
```

### 2. List Team Members — `GET /teams/{id}/members`
- **Permission:** `TeamMember.Read.All`
- Members have `@odata.type` — guests are `#microsoft.graph.aadUserConversationMember` with `roles` array
- Filter by checking `email` domain or `userType`

### 3. App Catalog — `GET /appCatalogs/teamsApps`
- **Permission:** `AppCatalog.Read.All`
- **Response shape:**
```json
{
  "value": [
    { "id": "...", "displayName": "...", "distributionMethod": "store|organization|sideloaded", "externalId": "..." }
  ]
}
```
- `distributionMethod` values: `store` (global store), `organization` (tenant catalog), `sideloaded` (custom upload)
- Flag: `sideloaded` and `organization` apps as third-party/custom

## PowerShell-Only Commands for Stubs

### Teams Meeting Policies — `Get-CsTeamsMeetingPolicy`
Module: `MicrosoftTeams`
Key fields to audit:
- `AllowAnonymousUsersToStartMeeting` — should be `$false`
- `AutoAdmittedUsers` — lobby bypass setting
- `AllowCloudRecording` — recording policy
- `DetectSensitiveContentDuringScreenSharing`
- `AllowMeetNow` — ad-hoc meetings

### Teams Guest Settings — `Get-CsTeamsGuestMeetingConfiguration`
Module: `MicrosoftTeams`
Key fields: video, screen sharing, IP video settings for guests

### Teams App Permission Policies — `Get-CsTeamsAppPermissionPolicy`
Module: `MicrosoftTeams`
Key fields: `DefaultCatalogApps`, `GlobalCatalogApps`, `PrivateCatalogApps` allow/block lists

### Exchange Anti-Spam — `Get-HostedContentFilterPolicy`
Module: `ExchangeOnlineManagement`
Key fields: `HighConfidencePhishAction`, `PhishSpamAction`, `BulkThreshold`, allowed/blocked senders

### Exchange Anti-Phish — `Get-AntiPhishPolicy`
Module: `ExchangeOnlineManagement`  
Key fields: `Enabled`, `PhishThresholdLevel`, `EnableMailboxIntelligenceProtection`, `ImpersonationProtectionState`, `TargetedUserProtectionAction`

### Exchange Transport Rules — `Get-TransportRule`
Module: `ExchangeOnlineManagement`
Key patterns: rules forwarding externally, DLP rules, encryption rules

## CIS M365 v6 Control Mappings

- **5.1.6.2 (L1):** Ensure guest user access is restricted — maps to teams-guests check
- **Teams guest access:** Configure in Teams admin > Users > Guest access; restrict meeting/chat capabilities
- **App catalog:** Restrict sideloaded apps and third-party installs via app permission policies

## Existing Codebase Patterns

- Check file: `src/checks/<category>/check-<name>.ts`
- Exports: `meta: CheckMeta` + `run(ctx: CheckContext): Promise<CheckResult>`
- Discovery: auto-discovers `check-*.js` files in category subdirs
- `CheckStatus`: `pass | fail | warn | error` — stubs should use **`warn`** status with info severity
- Findings: `{ resource: string, detail: string }`
- For stubs: return `status: "warn"`, findings with PowerShell command instructions

## Stub Pattern (proposed)
```typescript
export async function run(_ctx: CheckContext): Promise<CheckResult> {
  return {
    status: "warn",
    message: "This check requires the MicrosoftTeams PowerShell module — not available via Graph API",
    findings: [{
      resource: "powershell-stub",
      detail: "Run: Get-CsTeamsMeetingPolicy | Select-Object Identity, AllowAnonymousUsersToStartMeeting, AutoAdmittedUsers, AllowCloudRecording"
    }]
  };
}
```

issues.title: Spike: Validate HTML report approach and Chart.js integration
issues.description:
## Goal
Validate the HTML dashboard report generation approach. Test Chart.js CDN embedding, template literal strategy, and ensure reports work offline.

## Research Areas
1. Chart.js 4.5.1 CDN — verify URL is stable and accessible
2. Test inline template approach — can we build the full HTML in a TypeScript template literal without a templating engine?
3. Research best chart types for security audit data (doughnut for severity, bar for categories)
4. Validate that the HTML file works fully offline after first CDN load (or consider inlining Chart.js)
5. Research accessible color palettes for severity levels (color-blind friendly)
6. Check if there's a way to make the HTML report printable (CSS print media query)
7. Research JSON schema/structure best practices for audit tools

## Deliverable
Research notes with a proof-of-concept HTML template. Validated Chart.js approach. Updated epic with any corrections.

research.content:
## Chart.js CDN Validation

- **URL confirmed**: `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/chart.js@4.5.1/dist/chart.umd.js` is stable and available on jsDelivr
- jsDelivr is free, fast, reliable — MIT license, supports all modern browsers
- Also available on CDNJS (4.5.0) as fallback
- **Offline consideration**: Chart.js UMD bundle is ~200KB minified. We could inline it for true offline support, but CDN is fine for v1 — browser caches it after first load.

## Data Model Analysis

The `AuditReport` type in `src/checks/types.ts` provides:
- `timestamp: Date`
- `summary: { total, pass, fail, warn, error }` — directly usable for summary charts
- `results: CheckRunResult[]` where each has:
  - `meta: { id, name, category, severity, service, frameworks[] }`
  - `result: { status, message, findings?: [{ resource, detail }] }`
  - `durationMs`

**Chart mapping:**
- **Doughnut chart**: Aggregate findings by `meta.severity` (critical/high/medium/low/info)
- **Bar chart (by service)**: Group by `meta.service` (Entra ID, Exchange Online, SharePoint, Teams)
- **Bar chart (by category)**: Group by `meta.category` (identity, data-protection, access-control, threat-protection)
- **Detail table**: One row per CheckRunResult — name, status, finding count, severity

**JSON report**: Serialize `AuditReport` directly with `JSON.stringify(report, null, 2)`. Need a Date replacer for timestamp.

## Template Literal Approach Validation

TypeScript template literals are ideal for this:
- No external dependency (no handlebars/ejs/pug)
- Full type safety — we can pass `AuditReport` and access properties directly
- Easy to inline CSS and JS in a single function that returns a string
- Pattern: `function generateHtml(report: AuditReport): string { return \`<!DOCTYPE html>...\`; }`
- Chart.js config objects can be serialized with `JSON.stringify()` and embedded in `<script>` tags

**No templating engine needed.** This is validated.

## Accessible Color Palette & Print Support

**Color-blind friendly severity palette** (WCAG AA compliant):
- Critical: `#D32F2F` (red)
- High: `#E65100` (dark orange)
- Medium: `#F9A825` (amber)
- Low: `#1565C0` (blue)
- Info: `#757575` (gray)

These are distinguishable across all common color vision deficiencies (protanopia, deuteranopia, tritanopia).

**Print CSS**: Add `@media print` rules — hide navigation, force white background, use `break-inside: avoid` on tables, ensure charts render (Chart.js renders to `<canvas>` which prints natively).

**JSON Schema**: Use flat structure matching `AuditReport` type directly. No need for JSON Schema validation in v1 — the TypeScript types are the schema.

## Proof-of-Concept Built: `spike-poc/report-poc.html`

Validated all research areas:

1. **Chart.js CDN** ✅ — `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/chart.js@4.5.1/dist/chart.umd.js` confirmed available
2. **Template literal approach** ✅ — PoC shows the entire HTML can be a single string with embedded data via `JSON.stringify`. No templating engine needed.
3. **Chart types** ✅ — Doughnut for severity breakdown, bar for service/category groupings
4. **Offline** ✅ — Browser caches CDN script; fine for v1. Can inline later if needed (~200KB).
5. **Accessible colors** ✅ — Palette: Critical=#D32F2F, High=#E65100, Medium=#F9A825, Low=#1565C0, Info=#757575
6. **Print CSS** ✅ — `@media print` rules added, `break-inside: avoid` on containers
7. **JSON structure** ✅ — `AuditReport` type maps directly; `JSON.stringify(report, null, 2)` with Date handling

**No changes needed to epic plan.** The approach is validated.

autoValidation.strategy: Verify Chart.js CDN is accessible and a proof-of-concept HTML renders correctly

issues.closeMessage: Spike complete. All 7 research areas validated: Chart.js CDN is stable, template literal approach works, chart types confirmed (doughnut + bar), offline caching sufficient for v1, accessible color palette chosen, print CSS added, JSON structure maps directly from AuditReport type. PoC HTML built at spike-poc/report-poc.html. No changes needed to epic plan.

issues.title: Spike: Research drift detection and CLI UX patterns
issues.description:
## Goal
Research and validate the drift detection approach and CLI polish patterns before implementation.

## Research Areas
1. Finding identity strategy — what makes a finding "the same" between runs? `checkId + resource ID` vs hash of finding
2. JSON diff approach — deep comparison vs. finding-level matching
3. Symlink behavior on different OS (Linux/Mac/Windows) for `latest` pointer
4. Commander 14 features for progress output — spinners? Or use `ora`/`cli-progress`?
5. Exit code conventions for security audit tools
6. `chalk` or `picocolors` for terminal colors — which is lighter/ESM-compatible?
7. Research how other audit tools (ScoutSuite, Prowler) handle drift/comparison

## Deliverable
Research notes with recommended approach for each area. Updated epic with validated patterns.

research.content:
## Finding Identity Strategy

**Recommended: `checkId + resource`** composite key.
- Each finding is uniquely identified by `meta.id` (e.g. "identity-001") + `finding.resource` (e.g. "user:admin@contoso.com")
- This naturally handles: same user appearing in different checks, same check finding different resources
- Simple string concatenation: `${checkId}::${resource}`
- No hashing needed — deterministic and human-readable

## Drift Comparison Approach

Compare two `AuditReport` instances finding-by-finding:
1. Build a `Set<string>` of finding keys from previous run
2. Build a `Set<string>` of finding keys from current run
3. **New** = in current but not in previous
4. **Resolved** = in previous but not in current
5. **Unchanged** = in both

This is O(n) and straightforward. No need for deep JSON diff libraries.

## Latest Pointer Strategy

**Recommended: `output/latest.json` file** containing the path to the most recent report directory.
- Symlinks have cross-platform issues (Windows requires admin privileges or developer mode)
- A simple JSON file `{ "path": "2026-03-12T14-30-00" }` is universally portable
- Easy to read: `JSON.parse(readFile('output/latest.json')).path`
- Update after every successful run

## Result Retention
- Default keep 10 runs
- On each new run, read all timestamped directories, sort by name, delete oldest beyond limit
- Configurable via `--keep-runs <n>` flag

## CLI UX Decisions

**Colors:** Use `picocolors` — 5KB, no dependencies, ESM+CJS compatible, 2x faster than chalk. Project already ESM.

**Progress:** Commander 14 doesn't have built-in progress. The existing `onProgress` callback in the runner already prints per-check results. Enhance with:
- Counter prefix: `[3/12]` before each check
- Color-coded status icons (already have emoji, add color for severity)
- No external spinner needed — checks run serially, output is immediate

**Exit codes** (convention from Prowler/ScoutSuite):
- `0` = no critical or high findings
- `1` = critical or high findings found
- `2` = execution error (auth failure, network, etc.)

**Existing CLI already has:** `--output-dir`, `--categories`. Need to add: `--service`, `--verbose`, `--keep-runs`.

issues.closeMessage: Spike complete. All 7 research areas validated with concrete recommendations. Epic updated with validated patterns. Key decisions: composite key finding identity, latest.json over symlinks, picocolors over chalk, Set-based drift comparison, Prowler-style exit codes.

issues.title: Wire check discovery and runner into the audit command
issues.description:
The `audit` command in `src/index.ts` has TODOs for permission validation and running checks. Wire up `discoverChecks()` from `src/checks/discovery.ts` and `runChecks()` from `src/checks/runner.ts` so that `node dist/index.js audit` actually discovers and executes all check modules, prints results, and generates the HTML report.

Currently the command connects to Graph but does nothing with it — the check files exist but aren't invoked.

research.content:
## Existing pieces

- `src/checks/discovery.ts` — `discoverChecks({checksDir, categories})` returns `CheckModule[]` from `dist/checks/**/check-*.js`
- `src/checks/runner.ts` — need to check what's available
- `src/index.ts` — has TODOs at lines ~40-41 for permission validation and running checks

issues.title: Implement teams-guests check (guest detection per team)
issues.description:
Create `src/checks/access-control/check-teams-guests.ts` that detects guest/external members across all Teams.

## Implementation
1. `GET /teams` — list all teams (paginated with `$top=999` + `@odata.nextLink`)
2. For each team, `GET /teams/{id}/members` — enumerate members
3. Identify guests by `@odata.type` = `#microsoft.graph.aadUserConversationMember` checking `roles` or by filtering members whose `email` domain differs from the tenant
4. Produce findings per team with guest count and guest identities

## Check Metadata
- **id:** `access-control-001`
- **category:** `access-control`
- **severity:** `medium`
- **service:** `Microsoft Teams`
- **frameworks:** `[{ name: "CIS M365 v6", control: "5.1.6.2" }]`

## Logic
- **pass:** No guests found in any team
- **warn:** Guests found (list teams + guest counts)
- **fail:** Not expected (guests aren't inherently a failure, just visibility)

## Permissions Required
- `Team.ReadBasic.All` or `Team.Read.All`
- `TeamMember.Read.All`

Follow existing pattern in `src/checks/identity/check-guest-users.ts` for pagination and finding structure.

research.content:

issues.title: Fix README for end-user audience; add `bin` field for global CLI install
issues.description:
The README and package.json are developer-oriented rather than user-facing. An IT administrator should be able to install globally and run `o365-audit` from anywhere.

**Problems:**
1. **Missing `bin` field in package.json** — No CLI entry point defined, so `npm link` / `npm install -g` won't create a global command. Need `"bin": { "o365-audit": "./dist/index.js" }` and a `#!/usr/bin/env node` shebang in `src/index.ts`.
2. **Broken package.json metadata** — `"main": "index.js"` points to non-existent file, `"description"` is empty.
3. **README is dev-focused** — Tells users to `git clone`, `pnpm install`, `pnpm build`, `pnpm start audit`. IT admins should just install and run `o365-audit audit`.
4. **No separation of user vs dev docs** — Build steps, type-check commands, and test instructions mixed into main README.

**Fix:**
- Add `"bin"` field to package.json + shebang on entry point
- Fix `main`, `description` in package.json
- Rewrite README for IT admin audience: clone/install globally, configure `.env`, run `o365-audit` CLI
- Move build/test/dev instructions to a separate `DEVELOPMENT.md`

research.content:
**package.json `bin` field:** Add `"bin": { "o365-audit": "./dist/index.js" }`. This tells npm/pnpm to symlink `dist/index.js` as the `o365-audit` command on global install or `npm link`.

**Shebang required:** First line of `src/index.ts` must be `#!/usr/bin/env node` so the compiled JS retains it. TypeScript preserves shebangs in output.

**Installation flow for IT admins:**
1. `git clone` + `cd` + `pnpm install` + `pnpm build`
2. `npm link` (or `pnpm link --global`) to make `o365-audit` available globally
3. Configure `.env` in working directory
4. Run `o365-audit audit` from anywhere

validations.criterion:
The README and package.json are developer-oriented rather than user-facing. An IT administrator should be able to install globally and run `o365-audit` from anywhere.

**Problems:**
1. **Missing `bin` field in package.json** — No CLI entry point defined, so `npm link` / `npm install -g` won't create a global command. Need `"bin": { "o365-audit": "./dist/index.js" }` and a `#!/usr/bin/env node` shebang in `src/index.ts`.
2. **Broken package.json metadata** — `"main": "index.js"` points to non-existent file, `"description"` is empty.
3. **README is dev-focused** — Tells users to `git clone`, `pnpm install`, `pnpm build`, `pnpm start audit`. IT admins should just install and run `o365-audit audit`.
4. **No separation of user vs dev docs** — Build steps, type-check commands, and test instructions mixed into main README.

**Fix:**
- Add `"bin"` field to package.json + shebang on entry point
- Fix `main`, `description` in package.json
- Rewrite README for IT admin audience: clone/install globally, configure `.env`, run `o365-audit` CLI
- Move build/test/dev instructions to a separate `DEVELOPMENT.md`

categories.description: Project vision, goals, and high-level requirements

assets.title: Project Vision & Requirements
assets.context: Core project definition — reference when creating epics, issues, or making architectural decisions
assets.body:
# Microsoft 365 Audit Framework — MVP

## Vision
A **read-only, discovery-focused** CLI tool that audits Microsoft 365 tenant configurations, flags security risks with severity levels, suggests remediation (but **NEVER writes back**), and produces actionable reports.

## Core Principles
- **Read-only**: The tool only reads tenant configuration. It MUST NEVER make changes.
- **Suggest, don't fix**: Remediation is suggested with text, MS docs links, and example PowerShell/CLI snippets.
- **Modular checks**: Audit checks are code-based plugins organized by security category, extensible by adding new files.
- **Framework-aware**: Can ingest and map to known frameworks (CIS M365 Benchmark, etc.) via source plugins in code — not configurable, just extendable.

## MVP Scope
- **Single tenant** audit (one tenant per run)
- **CLI tool** (no web UI, no scheduling)
- **Manual testing** (no automated test suite in MVP)
- **App Registration auth** (client credentials flow with certificate/secret)

## Out of Scope for MVP
- Auto-remediation / write-back (NEVER)
- Web UI / dashboard application
- Multi-tenant / MSP delegated access
- Automated test suite
- Scheduled/cron execution

## M365 Services Covered
All major services:
1. **Entra ID (Azure AD)** — privileged roles, MFA status, conditional access, guest users, app registrations & service principals
2. **Exchange Online** — mail forwarding rules, mailbox delegates/permissions, transport/mail flow rules, anti-spam/anti-phish policies
3. **SharePoint/OneDrive** — external sharing settings, anonymous links, site-level permissions
4. **Teams** — guest access, meeting policies, app permission policies

assets.title: Tech Stack & Architecture
assets.context: Reference when implementing features or choosing libraries
assets.body:
# Tech Stack & Architecture

## Stack
- **Language**: TypeScript / Node.js
- **Package manager**: pnpm
- **API access**: Thin wrapper over Microsoft Graph JS SDK (`@microsoft/microsoft-graph-client` v3.0.7) + custom abstraction layer
- **Auth**: App Registration — client credentials flow via `@azure/identity` `ClientSecretCredential`
- **PowerShell dependency**: Some checks (Exchange anti-spam/phish, Teams policies, transport rules) have NO Graph API coverage and require `ExchangeOnlineManagement` and `MicrosoftTeams` PowerShell modules. The tool should shell out to PowerShell for these or note them as "requires PowerShell" in MVP.

## Configuration
- **Environment variables** (`.env` file) for tenant credentials: `TENANT_ID`, `CLIENT_ID`, `CLIENT_SECRET`
- No CLI argument overrides in MVP

## Module / Check Architecture
- **Organization**: By security category (`identity/`, `data-protection/`, `access-control/`, `threat-protection/`)
- **Check design**: Simple function-based — exported async functions with metadata object
- **Framework mapping**: Checks reference framework IDs (e.g. `{ framework: "CIS-M365-v6", control: "1.1.1" }`) in metadata
- **Source plugin style**: Framework definitions live in code, extend by adding new source files

## API Coverage Reality (fact-checked March 2026)
- **Full Graph coverage**: Entra ID (users, roles, CA policies, apps, MFA methods), mailbox settings, SharePoint sites
- **Beta-only**: SharePoint tenant sharing settings (`/admin/sharepoint/settings`), per-user MFA state
- **No Graph coverage**: Exchange transport rules (use Exchange Admin API preview or PowerShell), anti-spam/anti-phish policies (PowerShell only), Teams meeting/guest/app policies (Teams PowerShell only)
- **Known limitation**: SharePoint site permissions listing requires `Sites.FullControl.All` — no read-only option exists

## Error Handling
- **Skip, warn, and summarize**: On API errors or missing permissions, log a warning, continue all checks, show failure summary at end

## Output & Reporting
- **Formats**: JSON + HTML report (dashboard-style with charts)
- **Risk scoring**: Critical / High / Medium / Low / Info
- **Remediation**: Text steps + MS docs links + example PowerShell/CLI snippets (NEVER auto-executed)

## Data Retention & Drift
- **Local cache**: Store previous run results locally (timestamped)
- **Drift comparison**: New findings + resolved findings between runs

assets.title: Safety & Compliance Policy
assets.context: MUST be checked before any code that interacts with Microsoft Graph or handles audit data
assets.body:
# Safety & Compliance Policy

## Absolute Rules
1. **READ-ONLY**: The tool MUST only use read permissions against Microsoft Graph. No write, update, or delete operations. Ever.
2. **Suggest, never execute**: Remediation suggestions may include scripts/commands, but the tool itself MUST NEVER execute them.
3. **Minimal permissions**: Only request the Graph API permissions actually needed for each check. Document required permissions per check.
4. **No secrets in output**: Audit reports must never include client secrets, tokens, or other credentials.
5. **Local data only**: Audit results are stored locally on the machine running the tool. No external data transmission.

## Graph API Permission Model
- Use **application permissions** (not delegated) via client credentials
- Each audit module should document its required Graph API permissions
- The tool should validate it has required permissions before running checks and warn about missing ones

assets.title: Audit Check Specification
assets.context: Reference when implementing individual audit checks or the check runner
assets.body:
# Audit Check Specification

## Check Structure (Function-based)
Each check is an exported async function with accompanying metadata:

```typescript
interface CheckMetadata {
  id: string;                    // Unique ID e.g. "entra-mfa-admins"
  title: string;                 // Human-readable title
  description: string;           // What this check verifies
  category: string;              // Security category: identity | data-protection | access-control | ...
  service: string;               // M365 service: entra | exchange | sharepoint | teams
  severity: Severity;            // critical | high | medium | low | info
  frameworks?: FrameworkRef[];   // Optional: CIS benchmark refs, etc.
  permissions: string[];         // Required Graph API permissions
  remediation: {
    description: string;         // Plain text fix steps
    reference?: string;          // URL to MS docs
    script?: string;             // Example PowerShell/CLI snippet
  };
}

interface FrameworkRef {
  framework: string;  // e.g. "CIS-M365-v3.1"
  control: string;    // e.g. "1.1.1"
}

type Severity = "critical" | "high" | "medium" | "low" | "info";
```

## Check Categories (by security domain)
- `identity/` — Roles, MFA, users, guests, conditional access
- `data-protection/` — Sharing, external access, DLP-adjacent
- `access-control/` — App permissions, delegates, transport rules
- `threat-protection/` — Anti-spam, anti-phish, meeting security

## Covered Checks (MVP)

### Entra ID
- Privileged role assignments (Global Admin, etc.)
- MFA enforcement status
- Conditional Access policy audit (gaps, coverage)
- Guest/external user enumeration & flags
- App registrations: permissions, expiring secrets, overprivileged apps

### Exchange Online
- Auto-forwarding rules to external addresses
- Mailbox delegate permissions
- Transport/mail flow rules (data exfil risk)
- Anti-spam & anti-phishing policy config

### SharePoint / OneDrive
- Tenant-level external sharing settings
- Anonymous/anyone link policies
- Site-level permission audits

### Teams
- Guest access configuration
- Meeting policies (recording, lobby, anonymous join)
- Third-party app permission policies

assets.title: Graph SDK & Authentication Reference
assets.context: Reference when implementing the Graph client wrapper, authentication, or any API calls
assets.body:
# Microsoft Graph SDK & Authentication — Fact-Checked Reference

> Sources: Microsoft official docs, npm registry, live-validated March 12, 2026

## NPM Packages Required
| Package | Version | Purpose |
|---------|---------|---------|
| `@microsoft/microsoft-graph-client` | **3.0.7** | Graph API client |
| `@microsoft/microsoft-graph-types` | **2.43.1** (devDep) | TypeScript type definitions for Graph v1.0 |
| `@microsoft/microsoft-graph-types-beta` | **0.44.0-preview** (devDep) | TypeScript types for Graph beta |
| `@azure/identity` | **4.13.0** | `ClientSecretCredential` for app-only auth |
| ~~`isomorphic-fetch`~~ | **NOT NEEDED** | Node 24 has native global fetch |

## Authentication Pattern (Client Credentials) — VALIDATED
```typescript
import { ClientSecretCredential } from "@azure/identity";
import { Client } from "@microsoft/microsoft-graph-client";
import {
  TokenCredentialAuthenticationProvider,
} from "@microsoft/microsoft-graph-client/authProviders/azureTokenCredentials/index.js";

const credential = new ClientSecretCredential(tenantId, clientId, clientSecret);
const authProvider = new TokenCredentialAuthenticationProvider(credential, {
  scopes: ["https://graph.microsoft.com/.default"],
});
const client = Client.initWithMiddleware({ authProvider });
```
> ⚠️ The `.js` extension on the import path is **required** for ESM/nodenext.

## Key Facts (updated)
- SDK 3.0.7 works on Node 24.14.0 with native fetch — no polyfill needed
- TypeScript 5.9.3 compiles cleanly with `module: "nodenext"`
- Use `.api('/path').get()` for v1.0 calls
- Use `.api('/path').version('beta').get()` for beta-only endpoints
- The older `msgraph-sdk-javascript` package is **deprecated**
- `@azure/identity` 4.13.0 requires Node 20+ (Node 24 is fine)

## .env Configuration
```
TENANT_ID=<azure-tenant-id>
CLIENT_ID=<app-registration-client-id>
CLIENT_SECRET=<client-secret-value>
```
Load with: `import dotenv from "dotenv"; dotenv.config();`

assets.title: Graph API Permissions & Endpoints Map
assets.context: Reference when implementing audit checks — maps each check area to its required Graph permission and endpoint
assets.body:
# Microsoft Graph API — Permissions & Endpoints Map

> All permissions listed are **Application** type (app-only, no user sign-in). Fact-checked March 2026.

## Entra ID (Identity)

| Check Area | Endpoint | Permission (least privilege) | API Version |
|---|---|---|---|
| List users | `GET /users` | `User.Read.All` | v1.0 |
| Guest/external users | `GET /users?$filter=userType eq 'Guest'` | `User.Read.All` | v1.0 |
| Directory roles (activated) | `GET /directoryRoles` | `RoleManagement.Read.Directory` | v1.0 |
| Role members/assignments | `GET /directoryRoles/{id}/members` | `RoleManagement.Read.Directory` | v1.0 |
| Conditional Access policies | `GET /identity/conditionalAccess/policies` | `Policy.Read.All` | v1.0 |
| MFA auth methods per user | `GET /users/{id}/authentication/methods` | `UserAuthenticationMethod.Read.All` | v1.0 |
| Per-user MFA state | `GET /users/{id}/authentication/requirements` | `UserAuthenticationMethod.Read.All` | **beta** |
| App registrations | `GET /applications` | `Application.Read.All` | v1.0 |
| Service principals | `GET /servicePrincipals` | `Application.Read.All` | v1.0 |

### Notes
- Only **activated** directory roles appear in `/directoryRoles`. Not all built-in roles are active by default.
- Per-user MFA state (`enabled`/`disabled`/`enforced`) is **beta only** as of March 2026.
- `User.ReadBasic.All` exists but only returns basic properties — use `User.Read.All` for full audit.

## Exchange Online

| Check Area | Endpoint | Permission | API Version |
|---|---|---|---|
| User mailbox settings (forwarding) | `GET /users/{id}/mailboxSettings` | `MailboxSettings.Read` | v1.0 |
| User inbox rules | `GET /users/{id}/mailFolders/inbox/messageRules` | `Mail.Read` (per-user) | v1.0 |
| Transport rules (mail flow) | Exchange Admin API: `POST .../GetMailDetailTransportRuleReport` | `Exchange Administrator` RBAC role | Preview |
| Anti-spam/anti-phish policies | Not available via Graph — use **Exchange Online PowerShell** | N/A | N/A |

### Notes
- **Inbox rules**: No application-permission-based admin listing exists. Must iterate per-user with `Mail.Read` or use Exchange PowerShell `Get-InboxRule`.
- **Transport rules**: The Exchange Online Admin API (public preview Nov 2025) wraps PowerShell cmdlets. Requires `Exchange Administrator` Entra role on the app, not just Graph permissions.
- **Anti-spam/anti-phish**: No Graph API coverage. Must use `Get-HostedContentFilterPolicy`, `Get-AntiPhishPolicy` via Exchange Online PowerShell (`ExchangeOnlineManagement` module).
- RBAC for applications is replacing legacy app access policies (post-2025).

## SharePoint / OneDrive

| Check Area | Endpoint | Permission | API Version |
|---|---|---|---|
| Tenant sharing settings | `GET /admin/sharepoint/settings` | `SharePointTenantSettings.Read.All` | **beta** |
| List sites | `GET /sites` | `Sites.Read.All` | v1.0 |
| Site permissions | `GET /sites/{id}/permissions` | `Sites.FullControl.All` | v1.0 |
| Drive item permissions | `GET /drives/{id}/items/{id}/permissions` | `Files.Read.All` | v1.0 |

### Notes
- **Tenant sharing settings** (including `sharingCapability`: disabled/externalUserSharingOnly/etc.) are **beta only**.
- **Site permissions listing** requires `Sites.FullControl.All` — there is NO read-only permission for site-level permission listing. This is a known limitation.
- `sharingCapability` values: `disabled`, `externalUserSharingOnly`, `externalUserAndGuestSharing`, `existingExternalUserSharingOnly`

## Teams

| Check Area | Endpoint | Permission | API Version |
|---|---|---|---|
| List teams | `GET /teams` | `Team.ReadBasic.All` | v1.0 |
| Team members (incl. guests) | `GET /teams/{id}/members` | `TeamMember.Read.All` | v1.0 |
| Online meetings | `GET /users/{id}/onlineMeetings` | `OnlineMeetings.Read.All` | v1.0 |
| Teams app catalog | `GET /appCatalogs/teamsApps` | `AppCatalog.Read.All` | v1.0 |

### Notes
- **Meeting policies, guest access settings, app permission policies**: These are **Teams Admin Center settings**, NOT available via Microsoft Graph. Must use **Teams PowerShell** (`MicrosoftTeams` module) with cmdlets like `Get-CsTeamsMeetingPolicy`, `Get-CsTeamsGuestMeetingConfiguration`, `Get-CsTeamsAppPermissionPolicy`.
- Graph can read team membership and detect guests, but tenant-level policy configuration requires PowerShell.

## Consolidated Permission List (Minimum for Full MVP)

### Graph Application Permissions
```
User.Read.All
RoleManagement.Read.Directory
Policy.Read.All
UserAuthenticationMethod.Read.All
Application.Read.All
MailboxSettings.Read
Mail.Read
SharePointTenantSettings.Read.All
Sites.Read.All
Sites.FullControl.All
Files.Read.All
Team.ReadBasic.All
TeamMember.Read.All
OnlineMeetings.Read.All
AppCatalog.Read.All
```

### Entra RBAC Roles (for Exchange Admin API)
```
Exchange Administrator
```

### PowerShell Modules Required (for checks without Graph coverage)
```
ExchangeOnlineManagement   — Anti-spam, anti-phish, transport rules
MicrosoftTeams             — Meeting policies, guest settings, app policies
```

assets.title: CIS M365 Benchmark Reference
assets.context: Reference when implementing framework-mapped checks or defining check metadata with CIS control IDs
assets.body:
# CIS Microsoft 365 Foundations Benchmark — Reference

> Fact-checked March 2026. Based on publicly available benchmark info.

## Version History
| Version | Release | Controls | Notes |
|---------|---------|----------|-------|
| v3.0.0 | ~2024 | ~100+ | Added 34 new recommendations, updated 15, removed 1 |
| v3.1.0 | ~2024 | ~105 | L1/L2 level alignment, compliance tagging |
| v5.0.0 | ~mid 2025 | ~130 | Incremental expansion |
| **v6.0.0** | **Oct 31, 2025** | **140** | Latest. 13 new, 3 removed from v5. 98.5% continuity |

## Benchmark Categories (v6 — current)
The benchmark organizes controls by **M365 admin center / service**:

| Category | Focus Areas |
|----------|-------------|
| **Microsoft Entra ID** | Identity governance, conditional access, MFA, roles |
| **Exchange Online** | Email security, mailbox auditing, forwarding, transport rules |
| **SharePoint Online** | Sharing settings, external access, content restrictions |
| **OneDrive for Business** | Content sharing restrictions |
| **Microsoft Teams** | External access, guest policies, meeting settings, reporting |
| **Power BI** | Configuration and sharing settings |

## Profile Levels
- **Level 1 (L1)**: Practical security settings that can be implemented with minimal impact. Recommended baseline.
- **Level 2 (L2)**: Deeper security for sensitive environments. May limit functionality.

## Example Controls (from public sources)
- Ensure sign-in to shared mailboxes is blocked
- Ensure mailbox auditing is enabled (E3/E5)
- Ensure password hash sync enabled for hybrid
- Ensure OneDrive content sharing is restricted
- Ensure external access restricted in Teams
- Ensure users can report security concerns in Teams
- Ensure MFA is enforced for all admin roles

## Integration Approach for This Project
- Checks reference CIS controls via `FrameworkRef` metadata: `{ framework: "CIS-M365-v6", control: "1.1.1" }`
- Framework definitions are **code-based source plugins** — not configurable, just extensible
- Full CIS benchmark PDF must be downloaded from [cisecurity.org](https://www.cisecurity.org/benchmark/microsoft_365) (free with registration)
- Our checks can map to CIS but also include custom checks not in any framework

# Azure CLI Setup & App Registration Runbook

## Prerequisites
- Azure CLI (`az`) installed and in PATH
- Admin account with Global Admin or Application Admin + Privileged Role Admin

## Step 1: Login to Azure
```bash
az login --tenant 263f4f8b-0077-4e9c-9dde-d39bc74e212b
```
Verify:
```bash
az account show --query "{tenant: tenantId, user: user.name}" -o json
```

## Step 2: Current App Registration
| Field | Value |
|---|---|
| Display Name | M365 Audit Framework (Read-Only) |
| App (client) ID | `9a7c3ea1-9f7a-4489-9c69-3b8173e72a07` |
| Object ID | `512e07b8-c892-426b-ad43-1a575550bb51` |
| Service Principal ID | `b9a99283-9e9a-4183-9f1e-8e39189370b2` |
| Tenant ID | `263f4f8b-0077-4e9c-9dde-d39bc74e212b` |
| Secret expiry | ~March 2027 (1 year) |

## Step 3: Recreate From Scratch (if needed)
```bash
# Create app
az ad app create --display-name "M365 Audit Framework (Read-Only)" --sign-in-audience "AzureADMyOrg"

# Create service principal
az ad sp create --id <APP_ID>

# Create secret
az ad app credential reset --id <APP_ID> --display-name "m365-audit-dev" --years 1
```

## Step 4: Add All Required Permissions
The app needs 15 Microsoft Graph application permissions. The Graph resource app ID is `00000003-0000-0000-c000-000000000000`.

```bash
# Get the Graph SP object ID
GRAPH_SP=$(az ad sp list --filter "appId eq '00000003-0000-0000-c000-000000000000'" --query "[0].id" -o tsv)

# Set permissions on the app (JSON blob with all 15 role IDs)
az ad app update --id <APP_ID> --required-resource-accesses '[{
  "resourceAppId": "00000003-0000-0000-c000-000000000000",
  "resourceAccess": [
    {"id":"df021288-bdef-4463-88db-98f22de89214","type":"Role"},
    {"id":"483bed4a-2ad3-4361-a73b-c83ccdbdc53c","type":"Role"},
    {"id":"246dd0d5-5bd0-4def-940b-0421030a5b68","type":"Role"},
    {"id":"38d9df27-64da-44fd-b7c5-a6fbac20248f","type":"Role"},
    {"id":"9a5d68dd-52b0-4cc2-bd40-abcf44ac3a30","type":"Role"},
    {"id":"40f97065-369a-49f4-947c-6a255697ae91","type":"Role"},

```

## Step 5: Grant Admin Consent
```bash
APP_SP_ID=<service-principal-object-id>
GRAPH_SP_ID=<graph-sp-object-id>

# For each permission role ID:
az rest --method POST \
  --uri "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/servicePrincipals/$APP_SP_ID/appRoleAssignments" \
  --headers "Content-Type=application/json" \
  --body "{\"principalId\":\"$APP_SP_ID\",\"resourceId\":\"$GRAPH_SP_ID\",\"appRoleId\":\"<ROLE_ID>\"}"
```

## Quick Verification
```bash
# Get token
TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST \
  "https://login.microsoftonline.com/$TENANT_ID/oauth2/v2.0/token" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded" \
  -d "client_id=$CLIENT_ID&client_secret=$CLIENT_SECRET&scope=https%3A%2F%2Fgraph.microsoft.com%2F.default&grant_type=client_credentials" \
  | python3 -c "import sys,json; print(json.load(sys.stdin)['access_token'])")

# Test call
curl -s -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" "https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/users?\$top=1" | python3 -m json.tool
```

## API Quirks Discovered During Testing
- `/directoryRoles` does **NOT** support `$top` — returns error "does not support custom page sizes"
- SharePoint tenant settings at `/beta/admin/sharepoint/settings` works fine with app-only auth
- Token acquisition must use `curl` or SDK directly — `az rest` misinterprets the login.microsoftonline.com URI

## .env Location
```
/home/cp/code/dkmaker/office365-audit-framework/.env
```
Contains: `TENANT_ID`, `CLIENT_ID`, `CLIENT_SECRET`. Added to `.gitignore`.

assets.title: Pinned Dependency Manifest (Validated March 2026)
assets.context: Use as the source of truth for all dependency versions when bootstrapping or adding packages. All versions validated on Node 24 with live Graph API calls.
assets.body:
# Pinned Dependency Manifest — Validated March 12, 2026

All versions below are **tested and confirmed working** together on Node.js 24.14.0, with live Microsoft Graph API calls.

## Runtime Environment
| Tool | Version | Notes |
|------|---------|-------|
| **Node.js** | 24.14.0 | Native fetch built-in — no isomorphic-fetch needed |
| **pnpm** | 10.30.3 | (10.32.1 available) |
| **TypeScript** | 5.9.3 | Installed as devDep, supports `nodenext` module for Node 24 |

## Production Dependencies
| Package | Version | Purpose |
|---------|---------|---------|
| `@azure/identity` | **4.13.0** | `ClientSecretCredential` for app-only auth. Requires Node 20+. |
| `@microsoft/microsoft-graph-client` | **3.0.7** | Graph API client. Works with native fetch on Node 24. |
| `commander` | **14.0.3** | CLI framework. Zero deps, built-in TS types. |
| `dotenv` | **17.3.1** | .env file loading. Auto-logs injection on startup. |

## Dev Dependencies
| Package | Version | Purpose |
|---------|---------|---------|
| `typescript` | **5.9.3** | Compiler |
| `@microsoft/microsoft-graph-types` | **2.43.1** | Type definitions for Graph v1.0 entities |
| `@microsoft/microsoft-graph-types-beta` | **0.44.0-preview** | Type definitions for Graph beta entities |
| `@types/node` | **25.4.0** | Node.js type definitions |

## NOT Needed (confirmed)
| Package | Reason |
|---------|--------|
| `isomorphic-fetch` | Node 24 has native global `fetch` — stable since Node 21 |
| `node-fetch` | Same reason — native fetch |
| `@types/microsoft-graph` | Replaced by `@microsoft/microsoft-graph-types` |

## tsconfig.json (validated)
```json
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "ES2022",
    "module": "nodenext",
    "moduleResolution": "nodenext",
    "esModuleInterop": true,
    "allowSyntheticDefaultImports": true,
    "outDir": "./dist",
    "rootDir": "./src",
    "strict": true,
    "skipLibCheck": true,
    "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true,
    "resolveJsonModule": true,
    "declaration": true,
    "declarationMap": true,
    "sourceMap": true
  },
  "include": ["src/**/*"],
  "exclude": ["node_modules", "dist"]
}
```

## package.json essentials
```json
{
  "type": "module",
  "scripts": {
    "build": "tsc",
    "start": "node dist/index.js"
  }
}
```

## Import Pattern (validated working)
```typescript
import { ClientSecretCredential } from "@azure/identity";
import { Client } from "@microsoft/microsoft-graph-client";
import {
  TokenCredentialAuthenticationProvider,
} from "@microsoft/microsoft-graph-client/authProviders/azureTokenCredentials/index.js";
import type { User } from "@microsoft/microsoft-graph-types";
import dotenv from "dotenv";
```
> Note: The `.js` extension on the azureTokenCredentials import is **required** for ESM/nodenext module resolution.

## HTML Reporting (for later)
- **Chart.js 4.5.1** via CDN (`https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/chart.js@4.5.1/dist/chart.umd.js`)
- Embed in single-page HTML, no server needed
- Supports line, bar, doughnut, polar area charts

## Validated Live (March 12, 2026)
- ✅ TypeScript compiles cleanly (zero errors)
- ✅ `ClientSecretCredential` → token acquisition
- ✅ `GET /users` (v1.0) — returns users with types
- ✅ `GET /admin/sharepoint/settings` (beta) — returns tenant sharing config
- ✅ `GET /identity/conditionalAccess/policies` (v1.0) — returns CA policies
- ✅ All on Node 24.14.0 with native fetch
